what_it_does: Renders a 'shape how you're shown this' affordance in the address-help panel as a set of chips plus an optional arg input; it lets the operator choose a presentation preference (terser, more detail, lead with X, show as X) and calls onShape to record it.
observations: Splits preferences into BARE (terser, more) and ARG (lead_with, shape) arrays typed with as const.
Highlights the currently active kind with an ahelp-chip-on class and a star marker.
Shows a 'shaping…' badge and disables controls while the busy prop is true.
Arg-taking chips toggle an armed state that reveals an input and a submit button; Enter records, Escape clears.
Imports Surface, Badge, and EmptyState from ./kit but only Badge and EmptyState are rendered.
Includes an explicit design comment that the component intentionally carries no data-ui-ref so it does not overwrite the indicated locus.
Mirrors the backend PRESENTATION_PREFS vocabulary locally because /api/capabilities does not yet expose it.
standouts: The deliberate no-data-ui-ref decision, with a documented rationale about avoiding re-indication and orphaning.
The BARE/ARG split and the armed-input pattern for arg-taking preferences.
The active-kind visual feedback using a lit chip and star marker.
conventions: React functional component with local useState for armed kind and arg value.
Callback prop (onShape) delegates persistence to the parent/controller.
Shared kit components (Badge, EmptyState) and ahelp-* CSS class naming.
Disabled states during async busy; title attributes provide hints.
Const arrays with as const for kind literals.
concerns: Surface is imported from ./kit but never used in the rendered output.
fireArg is called with armed, which is typed string | null, even though it is only rendered when armed is truthy; this is a local type-safety gap.
The backend vocabulary is hardcoded here and could drift until capabilities expose it (already flagged in comments as G-F1FE-CAP).
notes: ShapeHow is the right-rail presentation-preference picker that records how an operator wants a locus shown, using bare chips and an armed arg input, while deliberately staying unaddressed to protect the indicated target.
questions: Where does the current pref come from and how is it refreshed after onShape?
Why is Surface imported if it is not used in this component?
How is the busy state coordinated with the POST /api/presentation-pref request?
What is the exact styling contract for ahelp-chip-on / ahelp-chip-armed?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file implements the visible preference-picker half of the described learning loop.
apparent_themes: Deterministic affordance kept separate from chat/voice path.
Self-unaddressed UI to preserve indication semantics.
Fail-loud backend validation with local vocabulary mirror.
Shared kit design system and responsive panel inheritance.
intention_signals: To give the operator a fast, persistent, point-at-locus control for teaching the system how to present a specific address, while ensuring the control itself never becomes the indicated target.
novelty: The 'shape how' affordance as a third sibling to annotate and wire-door, and the explicit no-data-ui-ref design rationale tied to indication integrity.
contribution: A self-contained, reusable preference picker that defines a small presentation-pref vocabulary, manages armed-input state, and emits structured onShape events for downstream recording; its documented decision to omit data-ui-ref contributes a constraint to the in-system feedback model.
summary_for_embedding: ShapeHow.tsx exports a React component that renders a 'shape how you're shown this' affordance with selectable presentation-preference chips and a conditional arg input, invoking an onShape callback to record the operator's preference.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to provide a deterministic, point-at-locus control for operators to teach the system how to present a given address, without displacing the indicated UI target.

symbols.does: Type alias for the current presentation preference, either null or a kind plus optional arg.
symbols.description: Pref is a simple union type used by the ShapeHow component to represent the currently selected presentation preference. It carries a string kind and an optional arg, matching the backend PRESENTATION_PREFS vocabulary.

symbols.does: React component that renders the presentation-preference picker.
symbols.description: ShapeHow receives the current preference, a busy flag, and an onShape callback. It renders bare and arg-taking chips, manages the armed arg input state, and invokes onShape when the operator selects or submits a preference.

symbols.does: Records a no-arg preference by calling onShape with its kind and a human hint.
symbols.description: fireBare handles clicks on the terser and more detail chips. It guards against busy state and passes the kind and a human-readable text hint to onShape, keeping the directive and the thread phrasing in one place.

symbols.does: Records an arg-taking preference after validating the input is non-empty.
symbols.description: fireArg handles submission of lead_with and shape preferences. It trims the typed arg, no-ops on empty input, calls onShape with the kind, arg, and a composed text phrase, then clears the armed state and input.
